title: Oxford Entrance Test
pageTitle: Only 4% Can Pass This Oxford Test
summary: Only 3% can pass this elite reasoning test.

homepage.title: Can You Pass This Oxford Entrance Test?
homepage.summary: Only 3% can pass this elite reasoning test.

infoPanel.title: About This Oxford Test
infoPanel.intro: This Oxford-style quiz is a fast reasoning challenge inspired by the kind of analytical thinking, pattern recognition, deduction, and careful reading often associated with tutorial-style discussion. It is not an official University of Oxford exam, but it is designed to feel sharp, focused, and mentally demanding.

columns.title: How It Works
columns.body: The quiz begins with a short confidence check, then moves through visual logic, attention, number patterns, word reasoning, deduction, lateral thinking, and final elite questions. Each round is short, so the test stays quick while gradually becoming harder.

columns.title: About Oxford Admissions
columns.body: Oxford undergraduate admissions are competitive and course-specific. Depending on the subject, applicants may be assessed through academic achievement, written work, admissions tests, interviews, references, and personal statements. There is no single universal Oxford entrance exam for every applicant.

infoPanel.footerBody: Every question in this quiz has one correct answer and an explanation. Your score is for entertainment and self-challenge only; it should not be treated as an official admissions result or academic assessment.

questions.prompt: Which item does not belong?
questions.explanation: Apple, orange, and banana are fruits. Carrot is a vegetable.

questions.prompt: What comes next?
questions.explanation: The numbers double each time: 3, 6, 12, 24, 48.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">3   6   12   24   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Choose the only number divisible by 3.
questions.explanation: 33 is divisible by 3 because 3 + 3 = 6.

questions.prompt: If CAT becomes DBU, then DOG becomes...
questions.explanation: Each letter moves forward by one: D to E, O to P, G to H.

questions.prompt: Which shape has one more side than a pentagon?
questions.explanation: A pentagon has five sides. A hexagon has six.

questions.prompt: Hand is to glove as foot is to...
questions.explanation: A glove covers a hand; a shoe covers a foot.

questions.prompt: All scholars read. Maya is a scholar. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If all scholars read and Maya is a scholar, then Maya reads.

questions.prompt: Which word is a palindrome?
questions.explanation: RADAR reads the same forwards and backwards.

questions.prompt: Which number is largest?
questions.explanation: 41 is the largest number.

questions.prompt: Which comes next in the day sequence?
questions.explanation: Dawn, morning, afternoon, evening, then night.
questions.visual: Dawn→Morning→Afternoon→Evening→?

questions.prompt: Which shape comes next?
questions.explanation: The shapes increase by one side: 3, 4, 5, then 6.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 96" style="width:260px;max-width:100%;height:96px"><polygon points="42,24 66,66 18,66" fill="#071b34"/><rect x="92" y="27" width="42" height="42" rx="6" fill="#071b34"/><polygon points="181,20 210,42 199,76 163,76 152,42" fill="#071b34"/><text x="234" y="62" font-size="35" font-weight="950" fill="#667085">?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which row does not belong?
questions.explanation: Row D has four shapes; the others have three.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 150" style="width:260px;max-width:100%;height:150px"><rect x="20" y="12" width="220" height="28" rx="10" fill="#f1f5f9"/><rect x="20" y="45" width="220" height="28" rx="10" fill="#f8fafc"/><rect x="20" y="78" width="220" height="28" rx="10" fill="#f1f5f9"/><rect x="20" y="111" width="220" height="28" rx="10" fill="#f8fafc"/><text x="130" y="34" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" fill="#071b34">◆  ◆  ◆</text><text x="130" y="67" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" fill="#071b34">●  ●  ●</text><text x="130" y="100" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" fill="#071b34">■  ■  ■</text><text x="130" y="133" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" fill="#071b34">▲  ▲  ▲  ▲</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which tile completes the checker grid?
questions.explanation: The grid alternates black and white squares, so the missing tile is black.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 210 210" style="width:210px;max-width:100%;height:210px"><rect x="20" y="20" width="50" height="50" rx="10" fill="#071b34"/><rect x="80" y="20" width="50" height="50" rx="10" fill="#eef3f8" stroke="#d7e0ea"/><rect x="140" y="20" width="50" height="50" rx="10" fill="#071b34"/><rect x="20" y="80" width="50" height="50" rx="10" fill="#eef3f8" stroke="#d7e0ea"/><rect x="80" y="80" width="50" height="50" rx="10" fill="#071b34"/><rect x="140" y="80" width="50" height="50" rx="10" fill="#eef3f8" stroke="#d7e0ea"/><rect x="20" y="140" width="50" height="50" rx="10" fill="#071b34"/><rect x="80" y="140" width="50" height="50" rx="10" fill="#eef3f8" stroke="#d7e0ea"/><text x="165" y="174" text-anchor="middle" font-size="31" font-weight="950" fill="#667085">?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: The arrow turns clockwise. What comes next?
questions.explanation: After up, right, and down, the next clockwise direction is left.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 88" style="width:260px;max-width:100%;height:88px"><text x="48" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="42" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">↑</text><text x="104" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="42" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">→</text><text x="160" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="42" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">↓</text><text x="216" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="38" font-weight="950" fill="#667085">?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which set breaks the pattern?
questions.explanation: The first three pairs match. The final pair contains two different diamonds.

questions.prompt: What comes next in the repeating pattern?
questions.explanation: The cycle is circle, square, triangle, then repeats.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 280 92" style="width:280px;max-width:100%;height:92px"><circle cx="35" cy="46" r="16" fill="#071b34"/><rect x="78" y="30" width="32" height="32" rx="6" fill="#071b34"/><polygon points="151,25 171,64 131,64" fill="#071b34"/><circle cx="207" cy="46" r="16" fill="#071b34"/><rect x="246" y="30" width="32" height="32" rx="6" fill="#071b34"/></svg>

questions.prompt: Which option completes the size pattern?
questions.explanation: The circles alternate small, large, small, large, then small.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 92" style="width:260px;max-width:100%;height:92px"><circle cx="38" cy="46" r="10" fill="#071b34"/><circle cx="88" cy="46" r="20" fill="#071b34"/><circle cx="146" cy="46" r="10" fill="#071b34"/><circle cx="196" cy="46" r="20" fill="#071b34"/><text x="238" y="58" font-size="34" font-weight="950" fill="#667085">?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which image has the odd symbol?
questions.explanation: C contains a star while the others are circles.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 86" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:86px"><text x="45" y="26" font-size="14" font-weight="900" fill="#667085">A</text><circle cx="45" cy="52" r="14" fill="#071b34"/><text x="115" y="26" font-size="14" font-weight="900" fill="#667085">B</text><circle cx="115" cy="52" r="14" fill="#071b34"/><text x="185" y="26" font-size="14" font-weight="900" fill="#667085">C</text><polygon points="185,34 190,47 204,47 193,55 197,69 185,61 173,69 177,55 166,47 180,47" fill="#071b34"/><text x="255" y="26" font-size="14" font-weight="900" fill="#667085">D</text><circle cx="255" cy="52" r="14" fill="#071b34"/></svg>

questions.prompt: Which missing shape keeps the pair rule?
questions.explanation: Each pair shows the same shape in black then white.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><circle cx="42" cy="45" r="16" fill="#071b34"/><circle cx="82" cy="45" r="16" fill="#eef3f8" stroke="#d7e0ea"/><rect x="142" y="29" width="32" height="32" rx="6" fill="#071b34"/><text x="214" y="56" font-size="34" font-weight="950" fill="#667085">?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which shape should come after two triangles?
questions.explanation: The pattern is circle, triangle, triangle, square, then repeats.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 90" style="width:340px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="170" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="25" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">○   △   △   □   ○   △   △   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Choose the option that is alphabetically first.
questions.explanation: Almond comes first alphabetically.

questions.prompt: How many letter S characters are in MISSISSIPPI?
questions.explanation: MISSISSIPPI contains four S characters.

questions.prompt: Choose the option with exactly five letters.
questions.explanation: Apple has exactly five letters.

questions.prompt: Which word appears twice?
questions.explanation: Blue appears twice.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 95" style="width:330px;max-width:100%;height:95px"><text x="165" y="31" text-anchor="middle" font-size="20" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">Red — Green — Blue — Yellow</text><text x="165" y="63" text-anchor="middle" font-size="20" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">Black — Blue</text></svg>

questions.prompt: If the instruction says choose option C, which option should you choose?
questions.explanation: The instruction explicitly says option C.

questions.prompt: Which number is repeated?
questions.explanation: 728 appears twice.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 88" style="width:320px;max-width:100%;height:88px"><text x="160" y="44" text-anchor="middle" font-size="23" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">483    728    619    728</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Ignore the largest number. Which remaining number is smallest?
questions.explanation: After ignoring 98, the smallest remaining number is 2.

questions.prompt: Which pair is identical?
questions.explanation: LOGIC / LOGIC is the only identical pair.

questions.prompt: Which colour is written in the middle?
questions.explanation: Green is in the middle of the three words shown.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 88" style="width:320px;max-width:100%;height:88px"><text x="160" y="44" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">Blue     Green     Red</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which option contains exactly two vowels?
questions.explanation: Claim contains two vowels: a and i.

questions.prompt: What comes next?
questions.explanation: The sequence doubles each time: 5, 10, 20, 40, 80.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">5   10   20   40   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: What comes next?
questions.explanation: This is the Fibonacci sequence: 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">1   1   2   3   5   8   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which number completes the sequence?
questions.explanation: The numbers are square numbers: 2², 3², 4², 5², then 6².
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">4   9   16   25   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: What comes next?
questions.explanation: The sequence lists prime numbers, so after 11 comes 13.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">2   3   5   7   11   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: What replaces the question mark?
questions.explanation: The differences are +3, +5, +7, +9, then +11.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">2   5   10   17   26   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: What number completes the grid?
questions.explanation: Each row shows a number, double it, then triple it.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 240 160" style="width:240px;max-width:100%;height:160px"><rect x="26" y="18" width="188" height="124" rx="18" fill="#f8fafc" stroke="#d7e0ea"/><path d="M88 18v124M152 18v124M26 59h188M26 101h188" stroke="#d7e0ea" stroke-width="3"/><text x="57" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="23" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">3</text><text x="120" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="23" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">6</text><text x="183" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="23" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">9</text><text x="57" y="86" text-anchor="middle" font-size="23" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">4</text><text x="120" y="86" text-anchor="middle" font-size="23" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">8</text><text x="183" y="86" text-anchor="middle" font-size="23" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">12</text><text x="57" y="128" text-anchor="middle" font-size="23" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">6</text><text x="120" y="128" text-anchor="middle" font-size="23" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">12</text><text x="183" y="130" text-anchor="middle" font-size="30" font-weight="950" fill="#667085">?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: If 5 notebooks cost £35, what do 8 notebooks cost?
questions.explanation: Each notebook costs £7, so 8 cost £56.

questions.prompt: The average of 7, 11, and x is 13. What is x?
questions.explanation: The total must be 39. Since 7 + 11 = 18, x is 21.

questions.prompt: A value falls from 120 to 90. What is the percentage decrease?
questions.explanation: The drop is 30 out of 120, which is 25%.

questions.prompt: What comes next?
questions.explanation: The terms are n × (n + 1): 1×2, 2×3, 3×4, and so on.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">2   6   12   20   30   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: If COLD becomes DPME, then WARM becomes...
questions.explanation: Each letter shifts forward by one.

questions.prompt: What letter comes next?
questions.explanation: The gaps increase by 1: +2, +3, +4, +5, then +6.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">A   C   F   J   O   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which word does not belong?
questions.explanation: Listen, Silent, and Enlist are anagrams. Stone is not.

questions.prompt: If BLUE = 4 and ORANGE = 6, then PURPLE = ?
questions.explanation: The number equals the number of letters.

questions.prompt: If stressed becomes desserts, then drawer becomes...
questions.explanation: The word is reversed.

questions.prompt: What letter is two letters after the letter before M?
questions.explanation: The letter before M is L. Two letters after L is N.

questions.prompt: Which word means the opposite of certain?
questions.explanation: Doubtful means unsure, which is the opposite of certain.

questions.prompt: Which phrase sounds most precise?
questions.explanation: The evidence suggests is the most careful academic phrase.

questions.prompt: Premise is to argument as evidence is to...
questions.explanation: Evidence supports a claim or argument.

questions.prompt: Which word is closest to “implicit”?
questions.explanation: Implicit means unstated but understood.

questions.prompt: All dax are vem. No vem are lop. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If every dax is a vem and no vem is a lop, no dax can be a lop.

questions.prompt: A is taller than B. C is shorter than B. D is taller than A. Who is tallest?
questions.explanation: D is taller than A, and A is taller than B and C.

questions.prompt: All roses are flowers. All flowers fade. Therefore...
questions.explanation: If all roses are flowers and all flowers fade, all roses fade.

questions.prompt: Some artists are poets. All poets are readers. Therefore...
questions.explanation: The artists who are poets must also be readers.

questions.prompt: Maya is before Leo. Leo is before Nina. Who is last?
questions.explanation: The order is Maya, Leo, Nina.

questions.prompt: I have cities but no houses, forests but no trees, and water but no fish. What am I?
questions.explanation: A map can show places without containing the real things.

questions.prompt: The more you take, the more you leave behind. What are they?
questions.explanation: Each step taken leaves a footprint behind.

questions.prompt: What can you break without touching it?
questions.explanation: A promise can be broken without physical contact.

questions.prompt: Every marble in Box A is blue. Some blue marbles are large. Are all marbles in Box A large?
questions.explanation: Some blue marbles being large does not prove all blue marbles are large.

questions.prompt: Only birds can enter the room. A robin enters the room. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If only birds can enter and a robin enters, the robin must be a bird.

questions.prompt: A bat and ball cost £1.10 total. The bat costs £1 more than the ball. How much is the ball?
questions.explanation: If the ball is 5p, the bat is £1.05, totaling £1.10.

questions.prompt: You pass the person in second place. What place are you now in?
questions.explanation: Passing second place puts you in second place.

questions.prompt: Three boxes are labelled Apples, Oranges, Mixed. All labels are wrong. Which labelled box should you sample first?
questions.explanation: Since the Mixed label is wrong, that box contains only one fruit type, revealing the rest.

questions.prompt: Given: X is not equal to Y. Y is equal to Z. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If Y equals Z and X does not equal Y, then X does not equal Z.

questions.prompt: Final pattern: what comes next?
questions.explanation: This is the look-and-say sequence.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 170" style="width:260px;max-width:100%;height:170px"><text x="130" y="28" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">1</text><text x="130" y="56" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">11</text><text x="130" y="85" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">21</text><text x="130" y="113" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">1211</text><text x="130" y="141" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">111221</text></svg>

questions.prompt: A clock loses 5 minutes every hour. If set correctly at noon, what time does it show after 6 real hours?
questions.explanation: It loses 30 minutes over 6 hours, so it shows 5:30.

questions.prompt: What comes next?
questions.explanation: This is the factorial sequence: 1!, 2!, 3!, 4!, 5!, then 6!.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">1   2   6   24   120   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Some A are B. No B are C. What must be true?
questions.explanation: The A that are B cannot be C.

questions.prompt: If P = 16 and CAT = 24, then DOG = ?
questions.explanation: Letters are converted to positions and added: D=4, O=15, G=7, total 26.

questions.prompt: What number completes the pattern?
questions.explanation: Square both numbers and add them: 6² + 7² = 36 + 49 = 85.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 270 130" style="width:270px;max-width:100%;height:130px"><text x="135" y="26" text-anchor="middle" font-size="21" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">3 + 4 = 25</text><text x="135" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="21" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">4 + 5 = 41</text><text x="135" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="21" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">5 + 6 = 61</text><text x="135" y="104" text-anchor="middle" font-size="21" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">6 + 7 = ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: What comes next in the sequence?
questions.explanation: The pattern adds 4, then 6, then 8, then 10, then 12.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">23   27   33   41   51   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which statement must be true? All glips are nors. No nors are taves.
questions.explanation: If every glip is a nor and no nors are taves, then no glips can be taves.

questions.prompt: If OXFORD is coded as PYGPSE, what happens to each letter?
questions.explanation: Each letter has moved forward by one: O to P, X to Y, and so on.

questions.prompt: Which option completes the visual pattern?
questions.explanation: The shape stays a circle while the fill alternates black, white, black, white.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 270 90" style="width:270px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><circle cx="42" cy="45" r="17" fill="#071b34"/><circle cx="96" cy="45" r="17" fill="#eef3f8" stroke="#d7e0ea"/><circle cx="150" cy="45" r="17" fill="#071b34"/><text x="214" y="56" font-size="34" font-weight="950" fill="#667085">?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Only essays with evidence are accepted. This essay was accepted. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If only essays with evidence are accepted, an accepted essay must have evidence.

questions.prompt: A candidate starts third and passes one person. What place are they in now?
questions.explanation: Passing the person in second place puts the candidate in second place.

questions.prompt: Which number is the odd one out?
questions.explanation: 16, 25, and 36 are square numbers. 48 is not.

questions.prompt: What is the safest conclusion? Some tutors are researchers. All researchers read papers.
questions.explanation: The tutors who are researchers must read papers.

questions.prompt: What comes next?
questions.explanation: This follows the look-and-say pattern after 111221.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 170" style="width:260px;max-width:100%;height:170px"><text x="130" y="28" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">21</text><text x="130" y="58" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">1211</text><text x="130" y="88" text-anchor="middle" font-size="22" font-weight="950" fill="#071b34">111221</text><text x="130" y="122" text-anchor="middle" font-size="34" font-weight="950" fill="#667085">?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Final question: which answer is most logically careful?
questions.explanation: A careful conclusion avoids overclaiming. 'May support' is the most precise option.
